name: Hugo Winterhalter
abstract: Hugo Winterhalter (August 15, 1909 – September 17, 1973) was an American easy listening arranger and composer, best known for his many arrangements and recordings for RCA Victor.

concerts.label: Pop Concerts in New York: Heart Fund Program

about_quotes.speaker: Hinton Milt
about_quotes.evidence: A great experience to play with some wonderful players, to play with [Unintelligible] and to play with Hugo Winterhalter.
about_quotes.doc_title: Hinton Milt — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Maxwell
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] And Hugo Winterhalter and there was a Latin guy down there, played. What was his name? I can't recall his name right now. [INTERVIEWEE] Yeah, I can visualize him. Herman Diaz.

about_quotes.speaker: Hayes Pillars
about_quotes.evidence: Count Basie had been through here and had given us several beautiful scores by Hugo Winterhalter, Buster Harding, and several other arrangers.

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Sauter
about_quotes.evidence: they had Hugo Winterhalter and me, and before that it had been Bill. That lasted about three weeks

about_quotes.speaker: Charles Calello
about_quotes.evidence: Now I grew up listening to Hugo Winterhalter who was a key arranger on RCA Victor.
about_quotes.doc_title: Charles Calello interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, July 21, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Harry Edison
about_quotes.evidence: And Hugo Winterhalter. But I think Buster Harding did more writing for the band...
about_quotes.doc_title: Harry Edison — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
